faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Newark home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small holes in wool clothing, carpets, or upholstery; silky tubes or cases on fabrics; adult moths flying around closets or storage areas; and larvae or cocoons in the folds of stored clothing. Our Newark technicians provide thorough inspections to identify the extent of any infestation.

faqs.question: What factors affect the cost of clothes moth control in Newark?
faqs.answer: Pricing ranges from $550 to $5,000 depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of your property, the number of rooms or areas affected, and whether follow-up treatments are needed. Historic Newark homes with extensive fabric furnishings may require more comprehensive treatment than smaller apartments.

faqs.question: Are clothes moth treatments safe for my family and pets in Newark?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products and methods that are safe when applied by our trained professionals. We take extra precautions in homes with children, pets, and sensitive individuals, and provide clear instructions for any necessary preparation or re-entry times after treatment.

faqs.question: How can I prevent clothes moths from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: Prevention includes regular vacuuming of closets and storage areas, storing woolens in airtight containers, washing or dry-cleaning garments before storage, maintaining low humidity levels, and scheduling periodic inspections. Our Newark team provides customized prevention plans based on your specific property and storage needs.
